title: 2 found dead in Derry, N.H., home
author: John Waller
bullets: Two 81-year-olds, Thomas and Donna Mosco, were found dead in their Derry, N.H. home on Friday.
The Derry Police Department confirmed the deaths are not considered suspicious and pose no threat to the community.
Concerned family members requested a wellbeing check after not hearing from the Moscos for an extended period.

title: NFL insider explained why Patriots-Eagles A.J. Brown trade could ‘happen quickly’
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: NFL insider Mike Garafolo suggests A.J. Brown could be traded from Eagles to Patriots soon after June 1.
The trade is delayed due to Eagles' financial strategy, potentially distributing $43.5 million dead money until 2027.
Previous trade talks involved the Rams, but their focus shifted to Davante Adams, opening doors for the Patriots.

title: A Scotsman is walking 3,200 miles across America for the World Cup in Boston
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Craig Ferguson, 21, walks 3,200 miles from Los Angeles to Boston for Scotland's World Cup matches and mental health awareness.
Ferguson's trek supports Scottish Action for Mental Health, raising £231,962 toward a £1 million goal for mental health support hubs.
The Glaswegian aims to finish the walk by June 13 at Boston Stadium, where Scotland will play Haiti and Morocco.

title: ‘Intoxicated patron’ allegedly assaults man, challenges responding officers to fight in Boston
author: John Waller
bullets: Kyle Corkum, 26, allegedly assaulted a man trying to escort him and challenged Boston officers to a fight.
Corkum faces multiple charges including strangulation, assault, and resisting arrest after an incident on Stuart St.
Police struggled to arrest Corkum as he physically resisted, shoving an officer and causing a confrontation.

title: Teen airlifted to Mass. hospital after bike jump goes wrong in N.H.
author: John Waller
bullets: A 17-year-old from Jaffrey, NH was airlifted to a Massachusetts hospital after a bike jump accident in New Ipswich.
The teen overshot a bike jump and landed on level ground; New Hampshire Fish and Game investigated the incident.
UMass Memorial LifeFlight transported the injured teen to UMass Memorial Medical Center in Worcester for treatment.

title: Plan an all-American summer getaway in these 6 New England towns, according to Forbes Travel Guide
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Forbes Travel Guide lists six New England towns as ideal for all-American summer getaways celebrating America's 250th birthday.
Highlighted destinations include Lexington, Mass.; Kennebunkport, Maine; Washington, Conn.; New Castle, N.H.; Watch Hill, R.I.; Barnard, Vt.
Accommodations offer unique historical experiences, such as the Inn at Hastings Park's Liberty tour in Lexington and Twin Farms in Vermont.

title: Livestream: Boston firefighter Robert Kilduff Jr. honored at funeral services today
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Funeral services for Boston firefighter Robert Kilduff Jr. held at the Cathedral of the Holy Cross.
Kilduff Jr., a 24-year veteran, died fighting a Dorchester fire on May 23, 2026.
Thousands, including Mayor Wu, attended; his children received a commemorative medal for his service.

title: 5 restaurants we can’t wait to try in June
author: Jillian Dara
bullets: Danny Meyer's Daily Provisions opens a new cafe in Seaport's Harborwalk offering breakfast, lunch, and dinner options.
The Grove at Lawn on D rebrands to a floral-themed venue with summer foods, drinks, and music events starting June 6.
Nubian Markets expands with a second location off Tremont Street, offering African-inspired dishes like crispy chicken + hoppin’ John.

title: For $579k, an unfinished 1800s carriage house condo
author: Regina Cole
bullets: 1800s carriage house condo at 8 Pine Street, Hyde Park listed for $579,000, unfinished inside.
Unit 4 is over 1,800 square feet with basics like studs and plumbing, monthly condo fee $110.
Potential uses include studio or home, requires variance for residential use; historic barn structure.

title: Christian Gonzalez’s teammate has his back amid reported contract holdout with Patriots
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Christian Gonzalez is absent from Patriots' OTA workouts amid contract negotiations reportedly seeking $32-35 million annually.
Teammate Marcus Jones supports Gonzalez's holdout for a lucrative contract extension with the New England Patriots.
Gonzalez, an All-Pro cornerback, was key in the Patriots' recent Super Bowl run and is under contract through 2027.
